kept.text:
A fast general purpose AI computer that doesn't need an accelerator.  
Nice article @DaveAltavilla

quoted_tweet.text:
The team at @Tenstorrent announced its new Galaxy AI platform today, built on the company’s @risc_v–based Blackhole accelerator architecture, underscoring an important shift in AI infrastructure.

Success at scale may not be defined by the fastest chip alone, but by how well compute, memory and networking work together, and how efficiently those systems scale from a single node to clustered deployments.

Galaxy is clearly aimed at that model. The strategy aligns with current market dynamics, but sustained performance at scale, software maturity and customer adoption will ultimately determine the success of this next phase for Tenstorrent. My full analysis is here, on @ForbesTech... 👇
https://t.co/57fU4v5Bn0

profile_bio.description: David Altavilla, Principal Analyst, HotTech Vision And Analysis - https://t.co/1zdlixN7GU, Forbes Senior Contributor,  EIC HotHardware - https://t.co/Hc4oRmJ235

value.string_value: Tenstorrent makes a case that AI infrastructure performance is not just about peak compute throughput. Instead, efficient AI systems depend on how quickly data moves.
Tenstorrent Unveils Galaxy AI Platform Targeting Scale And Efficiency

profile_bio.description: David Altavilla, Principal Analyst, HotTech Vision And Analysis - https://t.co/1zdlixN7GU, Forbes Senior Contributor,  EIC HotHardware - https://t.co/Hc4oRmJ235

kept.text:
excited to chat to anyone who will listen about the insanely niche and autistic uses i've had for my QuietBox this year! + To see what they've been cooking up + hanging out with the @tenstorrent team and seeing Jim on stage!

TT hardware + tt-lang, tt-forge and my trusted macbook is all i need in this world compute and software wise

profile_bio.description: God’s chosen principal engineer. What is impossible for you is not impossible for me.

kept.text: @FrameworkPuter Tenstorrent Blackhole module and I will consider it 🙏

profile_bio.description: God’s chosen principal engineer. What is impossible for you is not impossible for me.

kept.text:
@tenstorrent not be dramatic but id rather be all in on tenstorrent hardware and have an exclusive split of TT / apple stuff at home than using a single n*idia gpu because they simply hate their customers 

i got into TT hardware because it was open top to bottom and i agree with Jim's vision, the hw looked legit (and it is), and it looked like a tinkerer's paradise

I had my share of complaints about the software stack, how cluttered things seemed to be, etc...  but imo now that i've gotten back into it after a few months of break, it has been better than ever (that and the models are better than ever at writing eg tt-lang, too...)

lately i've been using tt-lang (python dsl to generate optimized kernels) and it's been a huge unlock (even just mentally... i never got along with tt-metal at all), getting some sick performance for these simulations I've been running, porting some weird finetunes of deepseek-prover and running 4 instances at once, using tt-mlir to add a tenstorrent backend to a Jax library etc

im also using the quietbox for the RAM, the 2x CPUs, the storage space at the same time, as the heart of my homelab, in general

it's been fun, i've been getting the performance i need and more, and there's so much i wanna do with it (including running qwen 3.6 27B (or a couple) on it... that'd be nice)

profile_bio.description: God’s chosen principal engineer. What is impossible for you is not impossible for me.

kept.text: @tinsmiu would you be interested in a very detailed explanation of how the tenstorrent blackhole p100a works? I could go on for hours...

profile_bio.description: 22 | part time long only pharma | part time unpaid tenstorrent dev | professional yearner

kept.text:
unfortunately tenstorrent has beat me to the punch with tt-lang (partially). a lot of time has been poured into witting a fully functional p100a emulator; there are a lot of pieces but i think it’s 90% functional right now

once that’s finished i can write a DSL that lowers directly to risc-v assembly (100% python). this is difficult right now because kernels cannot be debugged on the hardware and every kernel does at least 2-3x more work than tt-llk/sfpi would have you believe

then the tinygrad backend will be trivial

i’ll have better, more portable software than tenstorrent at the end of this

you’ll be able to run a tenstorrent card from macOS over eGPU

profile_bio.description: 22 | part time long only pharma | part time unpaid tenstorrent dev | professional yearner
